    Governance

    PII Protection

    Personally Identifiable Information detection, classification, and protection patterns for AI workloads using Microsoft Purview and Azure services

    ​
    🔒 PII Protection

    ​
    Overview

    Personally Identifiable Information (PII) represents any data that could identify an individual. In AI workloads, PII protection is critical for regulatory compliance, data security, and maintaining user trust. This document covers comprehensive patterns for detecting, classifying, and protecting PII in AI Landing Zones.

    ​
    PII Protection Overview

    ​
    Types of PII in AI Workloads

    CategoryExamplesRisk Level
    Personal IdentifiersName, SSN, Passport numberCritical
    Contact InformationEmail, phone, addressHigh
    Financial DataCredit card, bank accountCritical
    Health InformationMedical records, diagnosesCritical
    Biometric DataFingerprints, facial recognitionCritical
    Online IdentifiersIP address, cookies, device IDsMedium
    Professional DataEmployee ID, work emailMedium

    ​
    Data Classification Levels

    Data Classification Hierarchy:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  CRITICAL (L4)                                               │
│  • SSN, Passport, Financial Account Numbers                  │
│  • Health Records (PHI)                                      │
│  • Biometric Data                                            │
│  Action: Encrypt + Tokenize + Strict Access Control          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  SENSITIVE (L3)                                              │
│  • Personal Contact Info                                     │
│  • Government ID Numbers                                     │
│  • Financial Transaction Details                             │
│  Action: Encrypt + Access Logging                            │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  INTERNAL (L2)                                               │
│  • Employee Information                                      │
│  • Internal Communications                                   │
│  • Work History                                              │
│  Action: Access Control + Audit Logs                         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  PUBLIC (L1)                                                 │
│  • Published Information                                     │
│  • Marketing Materials                                       │
│  • Public Directory Listings                                 │
│  Action: Standard Security                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    ​
    Regulatory Drivers

    RegulationPII RequirementsPenalties
    GDPRConsent, right to deletion, data portabilityUp to 4% global revenue
    CCPA/CPRADisclosure, opt-out, deletion rightsUp to $7,500 per violation
    HIPAAPHI protection, minimum necessary, audit trailsUp to $1.5M per violation
    PCI DSSCardholder data protectionFines + card brand penalties
    SOXFinancial data controlsCriminal penalties

    Microsoft Purview Integration

    ​
    Purview Data Map for AI Data

    Microsoft Purview provides unified data governance across your AI Landing Zone: 
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                PURVIEW DATA GOVERNANCE                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│   ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐         │
│   │    Data     │  │  Sensitivity│  │   Policy    │         │

│   │             │  │             │  │             │         │
│   │ • Scans     │  │ • Classify  │  │ • Auto-label│         │
│   │ • Lineage   │  │ • Protect   │  │ • DLP rules │         │
│   │ • Catalog   │  │ • Monitor   │  │ • Alerts    │         │
│   └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘  └──────┬──────┘         │
│          │                │                │                │
│          └────────────────┼────────────────┘                │
│                           │                                 │
│                    ┌──────▼──────┐                          │
│                    │  AI Landing │                          │
│                    │    Zone     │                          │
│                    │             │                          │
│                    │ • APIM Logs │                          │
│                    │ • Model I/O │                          │
│                    │ • Vector DB │                          │
│                    └─────────────┘                          │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    ​
    Sensitivity Labels and Policies

    LabelDescriptionAuto-Apply Conditions
    Highly ConfidentialRegulated PII (PHI, SSN)Pattern match + context
    ConfidentialPersonal data, financialPattern detection
    InternalBusiness data, employee infoBusiness context
    PublicPublished informationNone
    # Example: Create sensitivity label for PII
New-Label -Name "PII-HighlyConfidential" `
  -DisplayName "Highly Confidential - PII" `
  -Tooltip "Contains sensitive PII requiring maximum protection" `
  -ContentType "File, Email" `
  -EncryptionEnabled $true `
  -EncryptionProtectionType "Template" `
  -EncryptionRightsDefinitions "Owner:View,Edit,Print,Reply,ReplyAll,Forward,Extract"

    ​
    Data Loss Prevention (DLP)

    Purview DLP policies for AI workloads: 
    DLP Policy Configuration:

Policy: AI-Gateway-PII-Protection
├── Scope: 
│   ├── APIM Request/Response Logs
│   ├── AI Model Input/Output
│   └── Vector Database Content
├── Conditions:
│   ├── Content contains: SSN, Credit Card, Passport
│   └── Confidence level: High (75%+)
├── Actions:
│   ├── Block transmission
│   ├── Encrypt at rest
│   ├── Alert security team
│   └── Log for audit
└── Exceptions:
    ├── Encrypted channels (TLS 1.3)
    ├── Authorized AI training pipelines
    └── Approved data processing agreements

    ​
    Insider Risk Management

    Purview Insider Risk Management detects potential data exfiltration:
    IndicatorDetection MethodResponse
    Bulk DownloadsUnusual volume of PII accessAlert + Block
    Off-Hours AccessAccess outside business hoursReview Queue
    Departing EmployeePII access before resignationEscalate
    Priority ContentAccess to executive PIIImmediate Alert

    ​
    PII Detection Patterns

    ​
    Native Model PII Detection

    Some AI models can detect PII in their responses: 
    <!-- APIM policy to request PII detection from AI model -->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- Add PII detection parameter to request -->
    <set-body>@{
      JObject body = context.Request.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
      // Request model to identify PII in response
      body["extra_body"] = new JObject(
        new JProperty("pii_detection", true),
        new JProperty("pii_categories", new JArray("email", "phone", "ssn", "address"))
      );
      return body.ToString();
    }</set-body>
  </inbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Purview PII Classification

    Purview can automatically classify PII across your data estate:
    PII TypeDetection MethodConfidence
    Social Security NumberRegEx + Validation95%+
    Credit CardLuhn check + Pattern99%+
    Email AddressPattern matching90%+
    Phone NumberFormat validation85%+
    AddressML-based NER80%+
    Person NameML-based NER75%+
    Date of BirthContext + Pattern70%+

    ​
    Custom PII Patterns

    Define organization-specific PII patterns: 
    {
  "customPiiPatterns": [
    {
      "name": "Employee-ID",
      "pattern": "EMP-[0-9]{6}",
      "description": "Internal employee identifier",
      "confidence": 90
    },
    {
      "name": "Customer-Reference",
      "pattern": "CUST-[A-Z]{2}-[0-9]{8}",
      "description": "Customer reference number",
      "confidence": 85
    },
    {
      "name": "Project-Code",
      "pattern": "PRJ-[0-9]{4}-[A-Z]{3}",
      "description": "Internal project identifier",
      "confidence": 80
    }
  ]
}

    ​
    Multi-Language PII Detection

    Purview supports PII detection in multiple languages: 
    // Multi-language PII detection
public class MultiLanguagePiiDetector
{
    public async Task<PiiResult> DetectPiiAsync(
        string content, 
        string language)
    {
        var client = new TextAnalyticsClient(
            endpoint, credential);
        
        var documents = new List<string> { content };
        
        // Detect PII entities
        var response = await client.RecognizePiiEntitiesBatchAsync(
            documents, 
            language: language);
        
        return new PiiResult
        {
            Language = language,
            Entities = response.Value[0].Entities.Select(e => 
                new PiiEntity
                {
                    Text = e.Text,
                    Category = e.Category.ToString(),
                    Confidence = e.ConfidenceScore,
                    Offset = e.Offset,
                    Length = e.Length
                }).ToList()
        };
    }
}

    ​
    PII Handling Strategies

    ​
    Redaction and Masking

    StrategyExample InputExample OutputUse Case
    Full Redaction”John’s SSN is 123-45-6789""John’s SSN is [REDACTED]“High-sensitivity PII
    Partial Masking”john.doe@email.com""j***.***@email.com”Email addresses
    Token Replacement”123-45-6789”<SSN\_001>Analytics, training
    Hashing”john.doe@email.com""a1b2c3d4…”Pseudonymization
    <!-- APIM policy for PII redaction -->
<policies>
  <outbound>
    <set-body>@{
      JObject response = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
      string content = response.ToString();
      
      // Redact SSNs
      content = Regex.Replace(content, @"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b", "[SSN-REDACTED]");
      
      // Mask email addresses
      content = Regex.Replace(content, 
        @"\b([A-Za-z0-9._%+-])[^@]*@([A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,})\b", 
        "$1***@$2");
      
      // Mask phone numbers
      content = Regex.Replace(content, 
        @"\b\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}\b", 
        "***-***-****");
      
      // Redact credit cards
      content = Regex.Replace(content, 
        @"\b\d{4}[\s-]?\d{4}[\s-]?\d{4}[\s-]?\d{4}\b", 
        "[CREDIT-CARD-REDACTED]");
      
      return content;
    }</set-body>
  </outbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Tokenization Approaches

    Tokenization Architecture:

┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     TOKEN VAULT                              │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│   Original Data              Token                           │
│   ─────────────              ─────                           │
│   john.doe@email.com    →    tok_abc123xyz                   │
│   123-45-6789           →    tok_ssn_789def                  │
│   555-123-4567          →    tok_phn_456ghi                  │
│                                                              │
│   Token Format: tok_<type>_<random>                          │
│                                                              │
│   Properties:                                                │
│   • Non-reversible (no algorithm to derive original)         │
│   • Format-preserving (same length, valid format)            │
│   • Vault-secured (Key Vault encryption)                     │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    // Tokenization service
public class TokenizationService
{
    private readonly ITokenVault _vault;
    
    public async Task<string> TokenizeAsync(
        string originalValue, 
        PiiType type)
    {
        // Generate unique token
        var token = $"tok_{type.ToString().ToLower()}_{Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N")[..12]}";
        
        // Store mapping in vault
        await _vault.StoreAsync(new TokenMapping
        {
            Token = token,
            OriginalValue = await EncryptAsync(originalValue),
            Type = type,
            CreatedAt = DateTime.UtcNow
        });
        
        return token;
    }
    
    public async Task<string> DetokenizeAsync(string token)
    {
        var mapping = await _vault.RetrieveAsync(token);
        if (mapping == null) return null;
        
        return await DecryptAsync(mapping.OriginalValue);
    }
}

    ​
    Synthetic Data Generation

    For AI training without real PII: 
    # Synthetic PII generation for testing/training
from faker import Faker
import json

fake = Faker()

def generate_synthetic_pii_record():
    return {
        "name": fake.name(),
        "ssn": fake.ssn(),
        "email": fake.email(),
        "phone": fake.phone_number(),
        "address": fake.address(),
        "dob": fake.date_of_birth(minimum_age=18, maximum_age=90).isoformat(),
        "credit_card": fake.credit_card_number(),
        "company": fake.company(),
        "job_title": fake.job()
    }

# Generate training dataset
synthetic_dataset = [generate_synthetic_pii_record() for _ in range(10000)]

    ​
    Data Minimization Patterns

    Data Minimization Flow:

Original Request           Minimized Request
────────────────           ─────────────────
"Hi, I'm John Doe, 
 SSN 123-45-6789, 
 email john@email.com.
 I need help with..."
         │
         ▼
    ┌────────────┐
    │   Detect   │
    │    PII     │
    └────────────┘
         │
         ▼
"Hi, I'm [PERSON], 
 SSN [REDACTED], 
 email [EMAIL].
 I need help with..."

    ​
    Implementation Patterns

    ​
    Pre-Processing Detection

    <policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- Extract request content -->
    <set-variable name="request-content" value="@{
      JObject body = context.Request.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
      return body?["messages"]?.Last?["content"]?.ToString() ?? "";
    }" />
    
    <!-- Send to Text Analytics for PII detection -->
    <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="pii-detection" timeout="10">
      <set-url>https://{{text-analytics-endpoint}}.cognitiveservices.azure.com/text/analytics/v3.1/entities/recognition/pii</set-url>
      <set-method>POST</set-method>
      <set-header name="Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key">
        <value>{{text-analytics-key}}</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-header name="Content-Type">
        <value>application/json</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-body>@{
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("documents", new JArray(
            new JObject(
              new JProperty("id", "1"),
              new JProperty("text", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("request-content", "")),
              new JProperty("language", "en")
            )
          ))
        ).ToString();
      }</set-body>
    </send-request>
    
    <!-- Check for high-confidence PII -->
    <set-variable name="pii-result" value="@(((IResponse)context.Variables["pii-detection"]).Body.As<JObject>())" />
    
    <choose>
      <when condition="@{
        var result = context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("pii-result", null);
        if (result == null) return false;
        var documents = result["documents"] as JArray;
        if (documents == null || documents.Count == 0) return false;
        var entities = documents[0]["entities"] as JArray;
        if (entities == null) return false;
        // Check for high-confidence PII
        return entities.Any(e => 
          (e["confidenceScore"]?.Value<double>() ?? 0) > 0.8 &&
          new[] { "SocialSecurityNumber", "CreditCardNumber", "PassportNumber" }
            .Contains(e["category"]?.ToString()));
      }">
        <return-response>
          <set-status code="400" reason="PII Detected" />
          <set-header name="Content-Type">
            <value>application/json</value>
          </set-header>
          <set-body>{
            "error": "Request contains sensitive PII",
            "message": "Please remove personal identifiers before submitting.",
            "code": "PII_DETECTED"
          }</set-body>
        </return-response>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </inbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Post-Processing Redaction

    <policies>
  <outbound>
    <!-- Detect PII in AI response -->
    <set-variable name="response-text" value="@{
      JObject body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
      return body?["choices"]?.First?["message"]?.["content"]?.ToString() ?? "";
    }" />
    
    <!-- Send to PII detection service -->
    <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="response-pii" timeout="10">
      <set-url>https://{{text-analytics-endpoint}}.cognitiveservices.azure.com/text/analytics/v3.1/entities/recognition/pii</set-url>
      <set-method>POST</set-method>
      <set-header name="Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key">
        <value>{{text-analytics-key}}</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-body>@{
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("documents", new JArray(
            new JObject(
              new JProperty("id", "1"),
              new JProperty("text", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("response-text", "")),
              new JProperty("language", "en")
            )
          ))
        ).ToString();
      }</set-body>
    </send-request>
    
    <!-- Redact detected PII from response -->
    <set-body>@{
      JObject body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
      string content = body?["choices"]?.First?["message"]?.["content"]?.ToString() ?? "";
      
      var piiResult = ((IResponse)context.Variables["response-pii"]).Body.As<JObject>();
      var documents = piiResult["documents"] as JArray;
      
      if (documents != null && documents.Count > 0)
      {
        var entities = documents[0]["entities"] as JArray;
        if (entities != null)
        {
          // Sort by offset descending to avoid index shifting
          var sortedEntities = entities
            .OrderByDescending(e => e["offset"]?.Value<int>() ?? 0)
            .ToList();
          
          foreach (var entity in sortedEntities)
          {
            int offset = entity["offset"]?.Value<int>() ?? 0;
            int length = entity["length"]?.Value<int>() ?? 0;
            string category = entity["category"]?.ToString() ?? "PII";
            
            if (offset >= 0 && length > 0 && offset + length <= content.Length)
            {
              content = content.Substring(0, offset) + 
                       $"[{category}-REDACTED]" + 
                       content.Substring(offset + length);
            }
          }
        }
      }
      
      body["choices"][0]["message"]["content"] = content;
      return body.ToString();
    }</set-body>
  </outbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Audit Trail Maintenance

    <policies>
  <outbound>
    <!-- Log all PII detection events -->
    <log-to-eventhub logger-id="pii-audit-logger">
      @{
        var piiResult = context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault<JObject>("pii-result", null);
        var documents = piiResult?["documents"] as JArray;
        var entities = documents?[0]?["entities"] as JArray;
        
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("timestamp", DateTime.UtcNow.ToString("o")),
          new JProperty("type", "pii-detection"),
          new JProperty("contractId", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("contract-id", "")),
          new JProperty("userId", context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("user-id", "")),
          new JProperty("entitiesDetected", entities?.Count ?? 0),
          new JProperty("entityTypes", entities?.Select(e => e["category"]?.ToString())?.ToList()),
          new JProperty("action", "redacted"),
          new JProperty("clientIp", context.Request.IpAddress)
        ).ToString();
      }
    </log-to-eventhub>
  </outbound>
</policies>

    ​
    Data Subject Request Handling

    // Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) handler
public class DataSubjectRequestHandler
{
    private readonly IVectorStore _vectorStore;
    private readonly IAuditLogRepository _auditRepo;
    
    public async Task<DsarResult> ProcessAccessRequestAsync(
        string dataSubjectId)
    {
        // Search all AI systems for subject's data
        var vectorResults = await _vectorStore.SearchAsync(
            filter: $"userId eq '{dataSubjectId}'");
        
        var auditLogs = await _auditRepo.QueryAsync(
            x => x.UserId == dataSubjectId);
        
        return new DsarResult
        {
            SubjectId = dataSubjectId,
            VectorStoreRecords = vectorResults,
            AuditLogEntries = auditLogs,
            GeneratedAt = DateTime.UtcNow
        };
    }
    
    public async Task<bool> ProcessDeletionRequestAsync(
        string dataSubjectId)
    {
        // Delete from vector store
        await _vectorStore.DeleteByUserIdAsync(dataSubjectId);
        
        // Anonymize audit logs (retain for compliance)
        await _auditRepo.AnonymizeAsync(dataSubjectId);
        
        // Delete any cached conversations
        await _cache.DeleteAsync($"conv:{dataSubjectId}");
        
        return true;
    }
}

    ​
    Architecture Patterns

    ​
    PII Vault Pattern

    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                      PII VAULT ARCHITECTURE                  │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│   AI Landing Zone                                            │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │              API Management (APIM)                   │   │
│   │                                                      │   │
│   │   Request ──▶ PII Detection ──▶ Tokenize ──▶ AI    │   │
│   │                      │                    │          │   │
│   │                      ▼                    ▼          │   │
│   │              ┌─────────────┐      ┌─────────────┐   │   │
│   │              │  Token Vault│      │   AI Model  │   │   │
│   │              │  (Key Vault)│      │             │   │   │
│   │              └─────────────┘      └─────────────┘   │   │
│   │                      ▲                              │   │
│   │   Response ◀── Detokenize ◀──────────────────────── │   │
│   │                                                      │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                              │
│   Components:                                                │
│   • Token Vault: Azure Key Vault with HSM-backed keys        │
│   • Encryption: AES-256-GCM for PII at rest                  │
│   • Audit: All token operations logged                       │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    ​
    Data Segregation by Sensitivity

    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                DATA SEGREGATION ARCHITECTURE                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                              │
│   ┌───────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐  ┌───────────────┐   │
│   │  Public Data  │  │ Internal Data │  │ Sensitive Data│   │
│   │  (L1)         │  │  (L2)         │  │  (L3-L4)      │   │
│   │               │  │               │  │               │   │
│   │ • Marketing   │  │ • Employee    │  │ • PII         │   │
│   │ • Docs        │  │ • Internal    │  │ • PHI         │   │
│   │ • General KB  │  │   Comms       │  │ • Financial   │   │
│   │               │  │               │  │               │   │
│   └───────┬───────┘  └───────┬───────┘  └───────┬───────┘   │
│           │                  │                  │            │
│           └──────────────────┼──────────────────┘            │
│                              │                               │
│                              ▼                               │
│                    ┌──────────────────┐                      │
│                    │  AI Model Access │                      │
│                    │                  │                      │
│                    │  L1: All models  │                      │
│                    │  L2: Internal    │                      │
│                    │  L3-L4: Secure   │                      │
│                    └──────────────────┘                      │
│                                                              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    ​
    Encryption at Rest and in Transit

    LayerEncryptionImplementation
    TransitTLS 1.3APIM SSL termination
    ApplicationmTLSService-to-service auth
    Data at RestAES-256-GCMStorage Service Encryption
    BackupAES-256Azure Backup encryption
    KeysRSA-4096Azure Key Vault HSM

    ​
    Key Management with Azure Key Vault

    // Key Vault for PII protection keys
resource keyVault 'Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults@2023-02-01' = {
  name: keyVaultName
  location: location
  properties: {
    sku: {
      family: 'A'
      name: 'premium' // HSM-backed keys
    }
    tenantId: subscription().tenantId
    enabledForDiskEncryption: true
    enabledForTemplateDeployment: true
    enabledForDeployment: true
    enableRbacAuthorization: true
    enablePurgeProtection: true
    softDeleteRetentionInDays: 90
    networkAcls: {
      defaultAction: 'Deny'
      bypass: 'AzureServices'
      virtualNetworkRules: [
        {
          id: subnetId
        }
      ]
    }
  }
}

// Key for PII data encryption
resource piiEncryptionKey 'Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/keys@2023-02-01' = {
  parent: keyVault
  name: 'pii-encryption-key'
  properties: {
    kty: 'RSA-HSM'
    keySize: 4096
    keyOps: [
      'encrypt'
      'decrypt'
      'wrapKey'
      'unwrapKey'
    ]
  }
}

    ​
    Compliance Alignment

    ​
    GDPR Data Protection

    GDPR ArticleRequirementImplementation
    Art. 5Principles (lawfulness, purpose limitation)Data minimization, purpose tagging
    Art. 6Lawful basisConsent management, legitimate interest
    Art. 17Right to erasureData deletion workflows
    Art. 20Data portabilityExport in machine-readable format
    Art. 25Privacy by designDefault PII protection
    Art. 32Security of processingEncryption, access controls
    Art. 35DPIARisk assessment for high-risk processing
    GDPR Compliance Checklist:

□ Data minimization implemented (collect only necessary PII)
□ Consent tracking for AI training data
□ Right to deletion workflows operational
□ Data portability API available
□ Privacy impact assessments documented
□ DPO contact information published
□ Breach notification procedures in place
□ Cross-border transfer mechanisms (SCCs)

    ​
    HIPAA Safeguards

    SafeguardRequirementAI Landing Zone Implementation
    AdministrativeSecurity management, workforce trainingAccess policies, training logs
    PhysicalFacility access, workstation securityPrivate endpoints, device compliance
    TechnicalAccess control, audit controls, integrityRBAC, audit logging, encryption
    OrganizationalBusiness associate agreementsContractual controls

    ​
    PCI DSS Considerations

    For AI workloads handling payment data: 
    PCI DSS Scope Reduction:

Before: AI system in PCI scope
┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐
│  Client  │───▶│   AI     │───▶│ Payment  │
│          │    │  System  │    │ Gateway  │
└──────────┘    └──────────┘    └──────────┘
                     ↑
                All in scope

After: Tokenization reduces scope
┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐
│  Client  │───▶│Tokenizer │───▶│ Payment  │
│          │    │ (In Scope)│   │ Gateway  │
└──────────┘    └────┬─────┘    └──────────┘
                     │
                     ▼
               ┌──────────┐
               │    AI    │
               │  System  │
               │(Out of   │
               │ Scope)   │
               └──────────┘

    ​
    Cross-Border Data Transfer

    MechanismUse CaseImplementation
    Standard Contractual ClausesEU→Third countriesAzure Data Processing Agreement
    Adequacy DecisionsEU→Approved countriesLeverage Azure regions
    Binding Corporate RulesIntra-group transfersEnterprise agreements
    Data ResidencyStrict localizationRegion-specific deployments
    // Data residency configuration
resource aiServices 'Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts@2023-05-01' = {
  name: aiServicesName
  location: 'westeurope' // EU data residency
  sku: {
    name: 'S0'
  }
  kind: 'OpenAI'
  properties: {
    customSubDomainName: aiServicesName
    publicNetworkAccess: 'Disabled'
    networkAcls: {
      defaultAction: 'Deny'
    }
  }
}
